name: Razorpay Payment Service — Load Testing & Capacity Planning
scenario: After all M22 optimizations (JVM tuning, indexes, connection pool, caching), validate that Razorpay's payment service actually meets SLOs under production-like load before Diwali sale. Build a Gatling simulation and capacity planner.

core.description: PaymentLoadSimulation extends Simulation: feeder (merchants.csv random), POST /api/payments scenario with status+responseTime checks. Load: atOnceUsers(10) → rampUsers(200).during(30) → constantUsersPerSec(100).during(120).
Assertions: p99.lt(200), p95.lt(120), successRate.gt(99.9), throughput.gt(90). All 4 assertions defined.
CapacityPlanner: safeCapacityPerPod(reqPerSec, cpuPercent) and podsRequired(target, safe, buffer) methods.
@SpringBootTest (3 tests): JVM metrics present, HikariCP metrics present, capacity math for 1500 req/s returns 7 pods. All 3 pass.
README: how to run simulation (mvn gatling:test command) and how to read the HTML report.

bonus.description: SpikeTestSimulation: normal → 10× spike → recovery profile. Separate assertions for spike phase (error rate < 1% during spike, recovery within 30s).
AuthFlow scenario: POST /auth/token (save JWT) → repeat 50× POST /api/payments → refresh token. details() assertions per request name.
GitHub Actions performance-gate.yml: runs load test after staging deploy, blocks prod deploy on assertion failure, auto-rollback on failure.

ai_policy_explanation: 5 hints. Gatling class structure: extends Simulation, HTTP protocol + scenario + load profile in static initializer {}. FeederBuilder.random(): each virtual user picks a random row from CSV (more realistic than circular). Assertions syntax: global().responseTime().percentile(99).lt(200) — 'lt' means 'less than', not 'less than or equal'. details("requestName"): assertion on a specific named request (not global). Capacity formula: safeCapacity = (observedReqPerSec / observedCpuPercent) × 100 × 0.80 — applies 80% headroom rule.
